crime.value: Theft of $100,000 unregistered bonds by trustee; theft of General's pepper-pots
crime.quote: next Tuesday he will be required to turn over $100,000 unregistered bonds to a young man about to come of age

victim.value: Young man coming of age owed trust funds; General Cox whose pepper-pots were stolen
victim.quote: turn over $100,000 unregistered bonds to a young man about to come of age, for whom he has been a trustee

closed_world.value: Urban setting centered around Raffles Holmes's apartment and the General's dining room
closed_world.quote: proceed cautiously to the sideboard, and replace thereon these two souvenirs

culprit_and_motive.value: Trustee 'Grouch' attempting to steal bonds; Raffles Holmes stealing pepper-pots to return grandfather's wrongs
culprit_and_motive.quote: Raffles Holmes atoned for at least one of the offences of his illustrious grandsire

resolution.value: Raffles Holmes prevents the bond theft and returns stolen pepper-pots with apology note
resolution.quote: Returned with thanks for their use and apologies for having kept them so long

investigator.value: Jenkins, narrator and companion to detective Raffles Holmes

suspect_architecture.value: Single primary suspect - trustee 'Grouch' with clear financial motive ($100,000 bonds due Tuesday), means (access as trustee), opportunity (control of assets)
suspect_architecture.quote: next Tuesday he will be required to turn over $100,000 unregistered bonds to a young man about to come of age

red_herring.value: Raffles Holmes's criminal inheritance and dual nature as detective/cracksman misdirects from his actually heroic mission to return stolen items
red_herring.quote: Detective and Amateur Cracksman by Birth

clue_fairness.value: Reader learns alongside narrator Jenkins - Grouch's multiple visits, his true identity, the bond deadline, all revealed as Holmes discovers them
clue_fairness.quote: I've had an anxious caller here five times already to-day

social_world.value: High society trustees managing inheritances for young heirs, with reputational stakes so high that 'eminent respectability' becomes a weapon
social_world.quote: a name of such eminent respectability that I fairly gasped

alibi.value: Holmes demonstrates alibi construction techniques to Jenkins, showing how witness testimony can be manipulated through strategic positioning
alibi.quote: an alibi if things go wrong with me. You'd have sworn I was in there just now, wouldn't you?

reveal_mechanic.value: Physical return of stolen pepper-pots with written note proves the case resolution, converting theft back to righteous restitution
reveal_mechanic.quote: Returned with thanks for their use and apologies for having kept them so long

media_and_audience.value: Private gentleman's club gossip and speculation shapes perception of crimes, with social reputation as currency among the elite
media_and_audience.quote: Well, General, heard anything further about your pepper-pots yet?

investigator_wound.value: Holmes carries burden of criminal grandfather Raffles's legacy, must prove worthiness by undoing ancestral crimes rather than creating new ones
investigator_wound.quote: Raffles Holmes atoned for at least one of the offences of his illustrious grandsire

_meta.title: ebook john kendrick bangs r holmes co
